steps.title: Extract data from the table
steps.content: The table provides three points: when $x = -11$, $y = 27 + \frac{11}{27}$; when $x = -1$, $y = 27 + \frac{1}{27}$; when $x = 11$, $y = 27 - \frac{11}{27}$.

steps.title: Calculate slope using two points
steps.content:
Use the first two points to find the slope:
$$m = \frac{\left(27 + \frac{1}{27}\right) - \left(27 + \frac{11}{27}\right)}{(-1) - (-11)} = \frac{\frac{1}{27} - \frac{11}{27}}{10} = \frac{-\frac{10}{27}}{10} = -\frac{1}{27}$$

steps.title: Find the linear equation
steps.content:
Using point-slope form with the point $\left(-1, 27 + \frac{1}{27}\right)$:
$$y - \left(27 + \frac{1}{27}\right) = -\frac{1}{27}(x + 1)$$
$$y = -\frac{1}{27}x - \frac{1}{27} + 27 + \frac{1}{27}$$
$$y = -\frac{1}{27}x + 27$$

steps.title: Find the x-intercept
steps.content:
Set $y = 0$ and solve for $x$:
$$0 = -\frac{1}{27}x + 27$$
$$\frac{1}{27}x = 27$$
$$x = 27 \times 27 = 729$$
That matches choice D — D is correct.
